skill.name: brainstorming-features
skill.description: Facilitates creative ideation sessions for mobile and web app features, generating structured ideas with user stories, technical considerations, and implementation suggestions. Use when planning new features, exploring product direction, generating app ideas, feature discovery, product brainstorming, or when user mentions 'brainstorm', 'ideate', 'app ideas', or 'feature suggestions'.
skill.summary: Facilitates creative ideation sessions for mobile and web app features, generating structured ideas ...
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown fences, template text, and example technical suggestions as risky patterns. Manual review found no executable scripts, no command execution instructions, no weak cryptography implementation, no browser storage access by the skill, and no prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples and fenced output templates. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands or execute Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code fence delimiters around documentation examples. No command syntax is presented as an operation to execute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in prose, headings, or template labels. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher selection, or security-sensitive implementation appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto code or advice. The matches are ordinary product-planning text, including words such as description, Medium, and Design.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage Access
low_findings.description: The browser storage finding is an example technical consideration that mentions IndexedDB and Dexie. The skill does not read, write, or request access to browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a generated feature example for a note-taking app. It is not executable code and does not direct data extraction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance finding points to a prioritization matrix entry that says low value, high effort features should be avoided. It does not ask for host, network, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is part of a product prioritization table. There is no system information gathering command or semantic intent.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to turn vague product goals into useful feature options. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured ideation, prioritization, and feature briefs.
actual_capabilities: Generates feature ideas for mobile, web, desktop, and PWA products.
Structures ideas with problems, target users, user stories, and success metrics.
Compares concepts using value, effort, feasibility, and implementation complexity.
Produces detailed feature briefs, concise feature lists, and phased roadmaps.
Supports problem-first, opportunity-first, competitive, and journey-based ideation.
limitations: It does not validate market demand with real users or analytics data.
Technical suggestions are conceptual and need engineering review before implementation.
Competitor analysis depends on context supplied by the user.
It does not create designs, prototypes, or production code.

use_cases.title: Plan an MVP
use_cases.description: Turn a broad product idea into a focused set of launch features with clear user value and complexity notes.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Workflow
use_cases.description: Identify friction points in a current user journey and generate practical features that reduce effort.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Discovery
use_cases.description: Create feature briefs with dependencies, data needs, and implementation risks before technical planning.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Starter Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Brainstorm 10 feature ideas for a web app that helps [target users] solve [problem]. Include a one-line description and complexity for each idea.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize MVP Features
prompt_templates.scenario: Narrow a broad feature set into a launch plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming-features workflow to propose an MVP for [product]. Rank each feature by user value, business value, effort, and implementation complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Explore a User Journey
prompt_templates.scenario: Find improvements in a specific workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the user journey for [workflow] in [app type]. Identify friction points, then generate features that improve completion rate and user satisfaction.

prompt_templates.title: Create Feature Briefs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for product and engineering review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create detailed feature briefs for the top 5 ideas for [product]. Include target users, user stories, technical considerations, dependencies, risks, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: Brainstorm checkout improvements for an early e-commerce platform.
output: One-click reorder for returning customers, with low complexity and clear repeat-purchase value.
Guest checkout express to reduce account creation friction, with medium complexity.
Cart recovery emails with incentives, with medium complexity and measurable revenue impact.

output_examples.input: Generate feature briefs for a fitness app focused on motivation.
output: Achievement streak tracker with milestone rewards, notification reminders, and retention metrics.
Workout challenge groups with shared goals, progress views, and social accountability.
Adaptive weekly goals based on recent activity, user preferences, and missed sessions.

output_examples.input: Help plan an MVP for a note-taking app for nursing students.
output: AI flashcard generation from notes to reduce study preparation time.
Clinical rotation checklist templates for repeatable task planning.
Offline-first note search to support studying in low-connectivity environments.

best_practices: Provide the product type, target users, current constraints, and success metrics before asking for ideas.
Ask for both feature briefs and a priority matrix when planning a roadmap.
Review technical assumptions with engineers before committing to implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not request generic ideas without a target user or product problem.
Do not treat generated complexity estimates as final engineering estimates.
Do not skip user validation for high-effort or strategic feature ideas.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates product feature ideas, user stories, technical considerations, complexity estimates, success metrics, and roadmap groupings.

faq.question: Can it help with both mobile and web apps?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports mobile, web, desktop, and PWA contexts when the user supplies enough product detail.

faq.question: Does it create implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on feature discovery, planning, and product structure rather than production code.

faq.question: How much context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide target users, product goals, current features, constraints, and the specific problem or opportunity.

faq.question: Can it prioritize ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rank ideas by value, effort, feasibility, complexity, dependencies, and expected impact.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only documentation templates and examples, with no executable commands or malicious instructions.
